name: Base Station Architecture
description: Sub-sector template for base station (eNB/gNB) architecture patents. Covers radio unit design, baseband processing, fronthaul/midhaul interfaces (eCPRI, ORAN), massive MIMO antenna systems, and disaggregated RAN architectures.
contextDescription: Base station architecture patents cover the hardware and system design of cellular infrastructure. The industry is transitioning from monolithic base stations to disaggregated RAN (O-RAN) with separate radio unit (RU), distributed unit (DU), and centralized unit (CU). Massive MIMO (64T64R, 128T128R) is standard for 5G. Key targets: Ericsson, Nokia, Samsung, Huawei (outside US), and emerging O-RAN vendors (Mavenir, Parallel Wireless). Cloud-RAN and virtualized baseband processing are growing areas. Per-unit value is very high ($10K-$100K per base station).
scoringGuidance: Every cellular base station implements these patents — millions deployed worldwide.
Massive MIMO antenna array patents are critical for 5G NR — 64+ antenna elements per panel.
O-RAN disaggregation patents affect the entire vendor ecosystem — high strategic value.
eCPRI fronthaul interface patents are essential for RU-DU connectivity.
Per-unit value is extremely high compared to handset patents ($10K-$100K per base station).

questions.question: What base station architecture does this patent address? Score 8-10 for disaggregated RAN (O-RAN, CU/DU/RU split) or cloud-native RAN, 5-7 for massive MIMO antenna system design, 3-4 for traditional monolithic base station, 1-2 for no specific architecture.
questions.reasoningPrompt: O-RAN and disaggregated RAN are the industry trends — highest future value.

questions.question: Does this patent address massive MIMO antenna systems (antenna array design, beamforming architecture, calibration)? Score 8-10 for novel massive MIMO architecture (128+ elements) or hybrid beamforming, 5-7 for standard massive MIMO improvements, 3-4 for basic multi-antenna, 1-2 for no MIMO aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Massive MIMO is mandatory for 5G NR — every 5G base station uses it.

questions.question: Does this patent address fronthaul or midhaul interfaces (eCPRI, CPRI evolution, functional split options)? Score 8-10 for novel fronthaul compression or split architecture, 5-7 for eCPRI improvements, 3-4 for general fronthaul design, 1-2 for no fronthaul aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: eCPRI fronthaul is a standardized interface — every disaggregated base station uses it.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with O-RAN Alliance specifications? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of O-RAN interfaces (O-RU, O-DU, O-CU), 5-7 for O-RAN compatible design, 3-4 for general open interface concepts, 1-2 for proprietary/closed architecture.
questions.reasoningPrompt: O-RAN is the dominant industry trend for RAN disaggregation — high SEP potential.

questions.question: Does this patent address baseband signal processing in the base station (LDPC/polar coding, FFT, precoding)? Score 8-10 for novel processing architectures or hardware acceleration, 5-7 for processing optimization, 3-4 for general signal processing, 1-2 for no baseband aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Baseband processing is compute-intensive — hardware acceleration is critical for 5G throughput.

questions.question: How detectable is this base station innovation? Score 8-10 for architecture visible in product documentation or O-RAN conformance, 5-7 for behavior observable via network signaling, 3-4 for internal hardware requiring physical access, 1-2 for algorithm-level only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Base station architecture is often documented in product specs and O-RAN conformance reports.
